mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports valuation expansion, so sustained easing predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03 during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate jump above the recent trend indicates deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's 3-year rolling IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDP and retail sales signals broad demand acceleration, which should lift cyclicals and aggregate equity earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone for predicting 60-day returns, or if positive returns do not exceed baseline in the top demand-acceleration quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production combined with weak housing starts signals a broad supply-side slowdown and margin pressure, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls month-over-month and HOUST is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if negative 20-day SPX returns are not concentrated in periods where both INDPRO and HOUST weaken, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation with stable or rising policy rates implies tighter real financial conditions, so equity multiples should compress and S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising faster than FEDFUNDS over the prior 2 months
regime_hint: inflation scare with delayed policy response
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising CPIAUCSL relative to FEDFUNDS do not show below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the inflation-rate surprise does not matter once FEDFUNDS is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth above trend signals resilient household income and consumption, which should support earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 100k over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if payroll expansion episodes do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than non-expansion episodes, or if the signal flips sign after accounting for revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while housing starts are weak, consumer spending is sustaining growth despite rate-sensitive sectors, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month average while HOUST remains below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the retail-sales-over-housing spread does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction is not stronger than RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, falling industrial production combined with rising unemployment marks a contractionary regime that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint contraction signal does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns during tightening regimes, or if the interaction loses significance once FEDFUNDS is removed.
